decisions.title: Prioritize Table & Explore V2 GA blockers over refactor work
decisions.context: Multiple GA-blocking issues surfaced (ENG-9277 Table improvements, ENG-9134 E2 defaults, ENG-9643 Table timezone bugs) alongside major refactor initiatives (ENG-9567 Fork codebase, ENG-9565 Code Refactor Setup). Need to sequence work to hit GA dates without destabilizing.

arguments.for: GA delivers customer value now; refactors enable velocity later but can wait 2-3 weeks. Current bugs undermine Nail the Core pillar.
arguments.against: Refactor work already in flight; context switching expensive. Delaying creates merge conflicts and increases technical debt window.

decisions.title: Approve TDMS REST upload architecture change (ENG-9645)
decisions.context: Current implementation collapses same-name channels across groups, breaking data integrity for customers with structured test data. Requires API contract change before wider adoption.

arguments.for: Data correctness is non-negotiable for Embed Deeper strategy. Early catch prevents migration pain later.
arguments.against: Changes TDMS ingestion contract; requires customer communication and possible breaking change coordination.

decisions.title: Greenlight ArbPB S3 Ingestion (ENG-9501 RFC)
decisions.context: New ingestion path for arbitrary protobuf data via S3. Enables high-volume edge device ingestion (Sift Edge product). RFC stage requires architectural approval.
decisions.blockedBy: Need clarity on overlap with ENG-9256 partition design work and whether this supports multi-tenant routing (ENG-9404 failures suggest gaps)

arguments.for: Unlocks Sift Edge scalability; addresses Build the Moat by enabling novel data sources competitors can't easily replicate.
arguments.against: Introduces third ingestion paradigm alongside TDMS and current streaming. Operational complexity and monitoring surface area grows.

decisions.title: Commit to npTDMS migration timeline (ENG-7822)
decisions.context: TDMS library migration has been in backlog. With TDMS issues emerging (ENG-9645) and customer engagement plans active (ENG-8086), need to lock migration window or defer indefinitely.

arguments.for: Current library showing cracks; modern npTDMS maintained and performant. Migration during active customer engagement creates forcing function for quality.
arguments.against: Risky during customer-facing work. Better to stabilize current state, complete engagement, then migrate in Q2 with full test coverage.

decisions.title: Resolve multi-tenant routing auto-switch failures (ENG-9404)
decisions.context: Resource links fail to auto-switch organizations, breaking cross-workspace collaboration. Impacts Embed Deeper pillar if customers can't seamlessly navigate shared assets.

arguments.for: Critical UX regression for multi-org customers. Single-day fix prevents escalation to support burden.
arguments.against: Explore pathname migration (ENG-9550) may conflict; could solve both together in coordinated change.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High fragmentation: 22 meetings with only 8 focus hours. GA crunch mode requires deeper blocks for eng reviews (refactor decisions, architecture RFCs).
alerts: Three 1:1s scheduled same day as architecture decision deadline (ENG-9501 RFC)
No 2+ hour focus blocks scheduled Thu-Fri despite 5 open critical bugs
Customer engagement plan review (ENG-8086) conflicts with Table GA meeting window
